name: Navalagamella, Fresnedillas de la Oliva, Robledo de Chavela – circular route
name_original: Navalagamella, Fresnedillas de la Oliva, Robledo de Chavela – ruta circular
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Navalagamella traverse a diverse landscape approximately 50 kilometers west of Madrid. The region features varied topography, including an elevation of 760 meters, offering ascents and descents. Cyclists encounter natural elements such as the Perales River, winding through oak forests, and the Cerro Alarcón Reservoir. This area provides a range of physical challenges suitable for road cycling. Best road cycling routes around Navalagamella The most popular road cycling route is Navalagamella, Fresnedillas de la Oliva, Robledo de Chavela – circular route, a 25.6 miles (41.2 km) trail that takes 2 hours 5 minutes to complete. This route offers varied terrain through the local countryside. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Colmenarejo Slides – Views of Valmayor Reservoir loop from Cerro Alarcón, a moderate 47.4 miles (76.3 km) path. It features views of the Valmayor Reservoir and varied landscapes. Local road cyclists also love the Villamanta Town Square – XXL Bicycle Sculpture loop from Navalagamella, a 47.3 miles (76.2 km) trail leading through diverse scenery, often completed in about 3 hours 26 minutes. Road cycling around Navalagamella is defined by the Perales River, extensive oak forests, and varied topography with significant elevation changes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult routes. The routes in Navalagamella are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 50 reviews. More than 5000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Navalagamella's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Navalagamella?
A: Navalagamella offers a wide selection of road cycling routes, with nearly 100 different tours available. This includes options for various skill levels, from easy rides to more challenging routes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Navalagamella?
A: The region around Navalagamella features diverse terrain, ideal for road cycling. You'll encounter varied topography, including ascents and descents, as the area is situated at an elevation of 760 meters. Routes often wind through lush oak forests, along the picturesque Perales River, and past the Cerro Alarcón Reservoir, offering a mix of natural beauty and physical challenge.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of road cycling routes in Navalagamella?
A: Road cycling routes in Navalagamella cater to a range of abilities. You can find easy routes for a relaxed ride, a significant number of moderate paths, and challenging options for experienced cyclists looking for more demanding climbs and descents. Overall, the komoot community rates the routes highly, with an average score of 4.3 stars.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in Navalagamella?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes in Navalagamella are circular, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. A popular example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e977179093/navalagamella-fresnedillas-de-la-oliva-robledo-de-chavela-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Navalagamella, Fresnedillas de la Oliva, Robledo de Chavela – circular route&lt;/a&gt;, which offers varied terrain through the local countryside.

Q: What historical or natural attractions can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: Navalagamella's road cycling routes are rich with points of interest. You can explore remnants of the Spanish Civil War, such as military architecture including fortifications and bunkers. Natural highlights include the Perales River with its historic mills, and the Cerro Alarcón Reservoir. Routes also pass by ancient bridges like Puente del Hoyo and Puente del Pasadero. For scenic views, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5155198" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valmayor Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/740607" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Seat of Philip II&lt;/a&gt; viewpoint.

Q: Are there long-distance road cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, Navalagamella offers several long-distance road cycling routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14987923" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Colmenarejo Slides – Views of Valmayor Reservoir loop from Cerro Alarcón&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 76.3 km path, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31933722" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villamanta Town Square – XXL Bicycle Sculpture loop from Navalagamella&lt;/a&gt; covers 76.2 km, both providing extensive rides through diverse landscapes.

Q: Are there routes that connect Navalagamella with other towns?
A: Absolutely. The road cycling network in Navalagamella is well-connected, linking the municipality with neighboring towns such as Majadahonda, Villanueva de la Cañada, Quijorna, Valdemorillo, Robledo de Chavela, and El Escorial. This allows for longer, more varied rides and opportunities to explore the broader region.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Navalagamella?
A: The diverse landscapes and varied topography of Navalagamella make it suitable for road cycling across multiple seasons. However, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures and conditions for exploring the region's routes, avoiding the peak heat of summer or the cooler, potentially wetter days of winter.

Q: Can I find routes with significant climbs or descents?
A: Yes, Navalagamella's elevated terrain, situated at 760 meters, ensures that many road cycling routes feature significant ascents and descents. For a challenging ride with substantial elevation gain, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39559426" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cruz Verde Pass – qas loop from Navalagamella&lt;/a&gt;, which includes over 1300 meters of climbing.

Q: Are there any routes that offer scenic views of reservoirs?
A: Yes, several routes provide beautiful views of the region's reservoirs. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14987923" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Colmenarejo Slides – Views of Valmayor Reservoir loop from Cerro Alarcón&lt;/a&gt; specifically highlights views of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5155198" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valmayor Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. The Cerro Alarcón Reservoir itself is also a prominent natural feature along many routes.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy the most about road cycling in Navalagamella?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 50 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, the scenic beauty of the Perales River and oak forests, and the range of routes suitable for different abilities. The interconnectedness with nearby towns and the presence of historical sites also contribute to a rich cycling experience.
tips: In Fresnedillas de la Oliva there is a very original and fun route that runs through the streets of the town: the route is called ''Casas con Vida''. In the Fresnedillas tourist office you can find a brochure with the marked route - on the route you will see a total of 86 paintings and a large mural (painted by the artist Elena Parlange). Some of the most beautiful images are located in hidden places that must be discovered.

Incredible place full of antennas and radio telescopes to listen to space. The antennas impress, for their size and for the work they do. Check their official website to see if the museum is open or not.
Navalagamella is known for its hiking trails and natural surroundings, perfect for outdoor activities. In addition, its historical and cultural heritage offers an enriching experience for visitors.
